description: The SigningBackend trait in secretx-core/src/lib.rs defines algorithm() as a synchronous fn — not async. The Pkcs11Backend implementation of algorithm() opens an HSM session and performs a blocking PKCS#11 C_GetMechanismInfo call to discover the algorithm. This blocks a Tokio executor thread (or the calling thread in sync contexts) for the duration of the HSM round-trip. The trait's doc comment does not warn that implementations may block. Callers in an async context who call .algorithm() on an Arc<dyn SigningBackend> have no way to know they need to wrap it in spawn_blocking. What goes wrong: calling algorithm() on a Pkcs11Backend inside a Tokio async task blocks the executor thread, degrading throughput for all other tasks on that thread. Fix: either make algorithm() async (breaking change to the trait), or add a doc comment to algorithm() stating 'implementations must not perform blocking I/O — callers may invoke this on an async executor thread', and fix Pkcs11Backend to cache the algorithm at construction time rather than querying the HSM on every call.
title: SigningBackend::algorithm() is sync but may block on HSM I/O
